request:
Nano Collective has relaunched its website with a complete redesign that reflects what the collective actually builds: privacy-respecting, local-first AI tools made for the developers who use them. We moved away from the previous soft, colourful look toward a bold, high-contrast "developer terminal" aesthetic — clean black-and-white layouts, sharp edges, monospace detailing, an electric-blue accent, and an animated ASCII-art backdrop — paired with fresh editorial typography. The new homepage tells a clearer story, leading with our core principles (Privacy First, Open Source, Local First), showcasing our flagship tools Nanocoder and Nanotune on dedicated landing pages, and surfacing live proof of a growing community through real-time GitHub, Discord, and Reddit stats and a feed of the latest discussions. Behind the scenes we added website analytics and a public growth page, a branded asset generator for consistent social imagery, smooth entrance animations, and a tightened, leaner codebase. The result is a site that looks and feels like the open-source, community-built, local-first movement it represents — and a strong foundation for announcing what comes next.

https://nanocollective.com
